Title: Catalog API - Get Products List
Steps: [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products with admin Bearer token and storeId={{STORE_ID}} query param; [WAIT] Wait for 200 response; [ASSERT] Validate response body structure; [ASSERT] Verify pagination metadata
Assertions: [STATE] Response status is 200 OK; [FORMAT] Response contains products array; [FORMAT] Each product object has id, name, code, imgSrc fields; [FORMAT] Response includes totalCount pagination metadata
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP response status 200; [NETWORK] Response time under 1000ms; [CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors in console
Failure_Signals: Non-200 status code; Missing products array in response; Absent totalCount field; 401 Unauthorized if token missing

Title: Catalog API - Get Product by ID
Preconditions: At least one product exists in catalog
Steps: [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products to fetch list; [ACT] Extract first product ID from response; [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/{id} with extracted ID; [WAIT] Wait for 200 response; [ASSERT] Verify full product detail fields
Assertions: [STATE] Response status is 200 OK; [FORMAT] Product contains descriptions, prices, images, variations fields; [FORMAT] SEO info object populated; [FORMAT] Category assignments array present
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP response status 200; [NETWORK] Response payload size reasonable (under 500KB); [CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: Non-200 status; Missing required detail fields; Empty or null SEO info

Title: Catalog API - Search Products
Steps: [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products?keyword=laptop; [WAIT] Wait for response; [ASSERT] Verify matching results returned; [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products?keyword=nonexistent_xyz; [WAIT] Wait for response; [ASSERT] Verify empty result set
Assertions: [STATE] Keyword search returns products matching 'laptop'; [STATE] Empty search keyword=nonexistent_xyz returns empty array; [MATH] totalCount=0 for no-match search; [FORMAT] Response structure consistent across both calls
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP status 200 for both requests; [NETWORK] Response time under 500ms; [CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: Status other than 200; Matching search returns empty array; Non-matching search returns results; Response time over 500ms

Title: Pricing API - Get Product Prices
Steps: [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/pricing/evaluate with product IDs array; [WAIT] Wait for 200 response; [ASSERT] Verify price structure; [ASSERT] Check ListPrice and SalePrice; [ASSERT] Verify currency code
Assertions: [FORMAT] Price objects returned for each product ID; [FORMAT] ListPrice field present and numeric; [FORMAT] SalePrice field present and numeric; [FORMAT] Currency code matches store configuration; [FORMAT] Price tiers array included if tier pricing configured
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP status 200; [NETWORK] Response includes all requested product IDs; [ADMIN] Prices match values set in Admin pricing module
Failure_Signals: Missing ListPrice or SalePrice; Currency code mismatch; 404 for valid product IDs

Title: Pricing API - Price Calculation with Quantity
Preconditions: Tier pricing configured for test products
Test_Data: admin_email={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}; admin_password={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}; test_sku={{TEST_SKU}}; qty_1=1; qty_10=10; qty_100=100
Steps: [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/pricing/evaluate with productId={{TEST_SKU}} and quantity=1; [ASSERT] Record per-unit price at qty=1; [ACT] POST with quantity=10; [ASSERT] Record per-unit price at qty=10; [ACT] POST with quantity=100; [ASSERT] Compare prices across quantities
Assertions: [MATH] Per-unit price at qty=10 is less than or equal to qty=1; [MATH] Per-unit price at qty=100 is less than or equal to qty=10; [FORMAT] Price breakdown shows tier details; [STATE] Tier pricing applied correctly
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP status 200 for all three requests; [NETWORK] All three requests complete; [ADMIN] Tier thresholds match Admin pricing configuration
Failure_Signals: Tier pricing not applied; Higher quantity has higher per-unit price; No tier detail in breakdown

Title: Inventory API - Check Product Availability
Steps: [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/inventory/products/{id}/availability with admin token; [WAIT] Wait for response; [ASSERT] Verify stock quantity field; [ASSERT] Check fulfillment center data; [ASSERT] Verify in-stock status flags
Assertions: [FORMAT] Response includes InStockQuantity numeric field; [FORMAT] FulfillmentCenter object present with id and name; [FORMAT] AllowPreorder boolean flag present; [FORMAT] AllowBackorder boolean flag present; [STATE] In-stock status consistent with InStockQuantity value
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP status 200; [NETWORK] Response time under 300ms; [ADMIN] Stock quantity matches Admin inventory module value
Failure_Signals: Response time over 300ms; Missing InStockQuantity; Missing FulfillmentCenter; 404 for valid product

Title: Inventory API - Reserve Stock
Steps: [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/inventory/products/{id}/availability; [ASSERT] Record initial InStockQuantity; [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/inventory/products/{id}/reserve with quantity=1; [WAIT] Wait for reservation confirmation; [ASSERT] Verify stock decreased; [ACT] POST to release reservation; [WAIT] Wait for release confirmation; [ASSERT] Verify stock restored
Assertions: [STATE] Stock reservation POST returns 200/201; [MATH] Available quantity decreases by 1 after reservation; [STATE] Reservation ID returned in response; [STATE] Release POST returns 200/204; [MATH] Available quantity restores to initial value after release
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP status 200/201 for reserve; 200/204 for release; [NETWORK] Both requests complete without timeout; [ADMIN] Inventory module reflects reservation and release
Failure_Signals: Reserve fails with 4xx; Quantity does not decrease after reserve; Stock not restored after release

Title: Orders API - Get Orders List
Steps: [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/order/customerOrders with admin Bearer token; [WAIT] Wait for 200 response; [ASSERT] Verify response structure; [ASSERT] Check pagination; [ACT] GET with status filter parameter; [ASSERT] Verify filtered results
Assertions: [STATE] Response status is 200 OK; [FORMAT] Each order object has number, status, total, currency fields; [STATE] Pagination works: skip/take params respected; [MATH] totalCount matches returned items count; [STATE] Status filter returns only matching orders
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP status 200; [NETWORK] Response includes expected pagination metadata; [ADMIN] Orders visible in Admin order management panel
Failure_Signals: Non-200 status; Missing order fields; Pagination params ignored; Status filter returns incorrect orders

Title: Orders API - Get Order by ID
Steps: [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/order/customerOrders; [WAIT] Wait for list; [ACT] Extract first order ID from response; [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/order/customerOrders/{id}; [WAIT] Wait for 200 response; [ASSERT] Verify full order details
Assertions: [FORMAT] Order contains lineItems array; [FORMAT] Shipping address and method info present; [FORMAT] Payment method info present; [MATH] Order total equals sum of lineItems + shipping - discounts; [FORMAT] createdDate and modifiedDate timestamps populated
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP status 200; [NETWORK] Response includes complete order object; [ADMIN] Order details match Admin view of same order
Failure_Signals: Missing lineItems; Order total math mismatch; Null timestamps; Missing shipping or payment info

Title: Orders API - Create Order
Steps: [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/cart/carts to create cart; [WAIT] Wait for cart ID; [ACT] POST to add product {{TEST_SKU}} to cart; [ACT] PUT cart with shipping address; [ACT] PUT cart with shipping method; [ACT] PUT cart with payment method; [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/order/customerOrders to create order from cart; [WAIT] Wait for order creation; [ASSERT] Verify order created
Assertions: [STATE] Cart created with generated ID (201); [STATE] Product added to cart successfully; [STATE] Order created with status 'New'; [FORMAT] Order number generated and non-empty; [FORMAT] Order includes all configured line items and addresses
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP 201 for cart; 200/201 for order; [NETWORK] All API calls succeed; [ADMIN] New order appears in Admin orders list
Failure_Signals: Cart creation fails; Product not added; Order creation returns 4xx/5xx; Order status not 'New'; Order number absent
Cleanup: delete created order via API; delete created cart via API

Title: Customer API - Get Customer Profile
Steps: [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token to authenticate as test user; [WAIT] Receive access token; [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/members/{userId} with user Bearer token; [WAIT] Wait for 200 response; [ASSERT] Verify profile fields; [ASSERT] Check contact and address info
Assertions: [FORMAT] Profile response contains email, firstName, lastName fields; [FORMAT] Phones collection present; [FORMAT] Addresses collection present; [FORMAT] Organizations array present if B2B user; [STATE] Returned userId matches authenticated user
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP status 200; [NETWORK] Response size reasonable; [ADMIN] Profile data matches Admin customer view
Failure_Signals: Non-200 status; Missing email or name; Empty addresses for user with configured address; 403 accessing another user's profile

Title: Customer API - Update Customer Profile
Steps: [ACT] GET current profile via GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/members/{userId}; [ASSERT] Record current firstName; [ACT] PUT {{BACK_URL}}/api/members with updated firstName='UpdatedTest'; [WAIT] Wait for 200/204 response; [ACT] GET profile again; [ASSERT] Verify updated value; [ACT] PUT with original firstName to restore
Assertions: [STATE] PUT returns 200 or 204 success; [FORMAT] Subsequent GET returns firstName='UpdatedTest'; [STATE] Other profile fields unchanged after update; [FORMAT] modifiedDate timestamp updated after PUT
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP 200/204 for PUT; [NETWORK] GET after PUT returns updated value; [ADMIN] Admin customer view reflects updated name
Failure_Signals: PUT returns 4xx; GET after PUT still shows old name; Other fields modified; modifiedDate not updated
Cleanup: restore original profile values via API

Title: Customer API - Create Organization
Steps: [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/members/organizations with name='TestOrg_API013', description, addresses; [WAIT] Wait for 200/201 response; [ASSERT] Verify organization created with ID; [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/members/organizations; [ASSERT] Verify org appears in list; [ASSERT] Check members list includes creator
Assertions: [STATE] POST returns 200 or 201; [FORMAT] Created org has generated non-empty id; [STATE] Org retrievable via GET /api/members/organizations list; [FORMAT] Org contains provided name and description; [STATE] Members list initially contains creator
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP 200/201 for POST; [NETWORK] GET list includes new org; [ADMIN] Organization appears in Admin B2B organizations panel
Failure_Signals: POST returns 4xx; Created org has no ID; Org not retrievable after creation; Creator not in members list

Title: Authentication API - Login
Steps: [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with grant_type=password, username={{USER_EMAIL}}, password={{USER_PASSWORD}}; [WAIT] Wait for token response; [ASSERT] Verify token fields; [ASSERT] Check expiration; [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/members/{userId} with returned Bearer token; [ASSERT] Verify authenticated request succeeds
Assertions: [FORMAT] Response contains access_token non-empty string; [FORMAT] token_type equals 'Bearer'; [MATH] expires_in value greater than 0; [STATE] Authenticated GET request using returned token returns 200
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP 200 for token endpoint; [NETWORK] Token usable in Authorization header; [CONSOLE] No auth errors in console
Failure_Signals: Non-200 for valid credentials; Missing access_token; token_type not Bearer; Token rejected on authenticated request

Title: Authentication API - Invalid Credentials
Steps: [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with valid email + wrong password; [WAIT] Wait for error response; [ASSERT] Verify error status and body; [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with nonexistent email; [WAIT] Wait for error response; [ASSERT] Verify error response
Assertions: [STATE] Response returns 400 Bad Request for wrong password; [STATE] Error message does not reveal whether user exists (anti-enumeration); [STATE] No access_token in error response; [FORMAT] Response body does not contain stack traces or internal error details
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP 400 for both invalid attempts; [NETWORK] No tokens issued; [CONSOLE] No server errors leaked to response
Failure_Signals: Status 200 returned for invalid credentials; access_token present in error response; Stack trace exposed in response body; User existence revealed in error message

Title: Authentication API - Token Refresh
Preconditions: Valid refresh token obtained from login
Steps: [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with password grant to obtain refresh_token; [WAIT] Record access_token and refresh_token; [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with grant_type=refresh_token and the refresh_token; [WAIT] Wait for new token response; [ASSERT] Verify new access token issued; [NAV] Authenticated GET using new access_token; [ASSERT] Verify request succeeds
Assertions: [FORMAT] Refresh POST returns new access_token; [FORMAT] New refresh_token issued; [STATE] Authenticated request with new access_token returns 200; [STATE] Old access_token may still work until its expiry window
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP 200 for refresh POST; [NETWORK] New token accepted on subsequent authenticated request; [CONSOLE] No auth errors
Failure_Signals: Refresh POST returns 4xx; No new access_token returned; New token rejected on authenticated request

Title: Store API - Get Store Settings
Steps: [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/stores/{{STORE_ID}} with admin Bearer token; [WAIT] Wait for 200 response; [ASSERT] Verify store configuration fields; [ASSERT] Check currency settings; [ASSERT] Check language settings
Assertions: [FORMAT] Store data returned with id, name, url; [FORMAT] defaultCurrency field set; [FORMAT] currencies array lists available currencies; [FORMAT] defaultLanguage field set; [FORMAT] languages array lists available languages; [FORMAT] seoInfo object populated
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP status 200; [NETWORK] Response includes all store config fields; [ADMIN] Store settings match Admin Store management view
Failure_Signals: Non-200 status; Missing defaultCurrency or defaultLanguage; Empty currencies or languages arrays

Title: Catalog API - Create Product
Steps: [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products with name='TestProduct_API018', code='TEST-API018', categoryId; [WAIT] Wait for 200/201 response; [ASSERT] Verify product created; [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/{newId}; [ASSERT] Verify product retrievable; [ACT] DELETE {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/{newId} to clean up
Assertions: [STATE] POST returns 200 or 201; [FORMAT] Created product has generated non-empty id; [STATE] Product retrievable by new ID via GET; [STATE] Product appears in category product list; [STATE] DELETE for cleanup returns 200/204
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP 200/201 for POST; [NETWORK] GET by new ID succeeds; [ADMIN] Product appears in Admin catalog
Failure_Signals: POST returns 4xx; Created product has no id; Product not retrievable after creation; Cleanup DELETE fails

Title: Catalog API - Update Product
Steps: [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/{{TEST_SKU}} to retrieve current values; [ASSERT] Record current name and description; [ACT] PUT {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products with updated name and description; [WAIT] Wait for success response; [NAV] GET product again; [ASSERT] Verify changes saved; [ACT] PUT with original values to restore
Assertions: [STATE] PUT returns success (200/204); [FORMAT] Subsequent GET returns updated name and description; [STATE] Other product fields unchanged; [FORMAT] modifiedDate updated after PUT
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP 200/204 for PUT; [NETWORK] GET after PUT reflects new values; [ADMIN] Updated product name visible in Admin catalog
Failure_Signals: PUT returns 4xx; GET still shows old name after update; Other fields changed; modifiedDate not updated
Cleanup: restore original product values via API

Title: Cart API - Create and Manage Cart
Steps: [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/cart/carts to create new cart; [WAIT] Receive cart ID; [ACT] POST to add {{TEST_SKU}} item to cart; [ASSERT] Verify item added with correct price; [ACT] PUT cart item with updated quantity=2; [ASSERT] Verify quantity reflected in totals; [NAV] GET cart totals; [ASSERT] Verify totals; [ACT] DELETE cart item; [ASSERT] Verify item removed; [ACT] DELETE cart
Assertions: [STATE] Cart POST returns 201 with id; [FORMAT] Added item has correct price from pricing API; [MATH] Totals after quantity=2 equal 2x unit price + tax; [MATH] subtotal, tax, total fields calculate correctly; [STATE] Item removal reduces cart to empty; [STATE] Cart DELETE returns 200/204
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP 201 for cart create; 200 for item operations; [NETWORK] Totals consistent across requests; [ADMIN] Cart visible in Admin if needed
Failure_Signals: Cart creation fails; Item price incorrect; Totals math incorrect; Item removal fails; Cart delete fails

Title: Cart API - Apply Coupon
Preconditions: Cart with items; valid coupon code configured
Steps: [ACT] Create cart and add item {{TEST_SKU}}; [WAIT] Confirm items added; [NAV] GET cart totals before coupon; [ASSERT] Record pre-coupon total; [ACT] POST coupon code 'TESTCOUPON10' to cart; [WAIT] Wait for coupon application; [NAV] GET cart totals after coupon; [ASSERT] Verify discount applied; [ACT] DELETE coupon from cart; [ASSERT] Verify totals restored
Assertions: [STATE] Coupon POST returns 200/201 success; [MATH] Post-coupon total is less than pre-coupon total; [FORMAT] Discount amount shown in totals breakdown; [STATE] Coupon removal returns 200/204; [MATH] Totals after removal match original pre-coupon values
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP 200 for coupon operations; [NETWORK] Discount reflected in totals response; [ADMIN] Coupon usage count incremented in Admin promotions
Failure_Signals: Coupon POST returns 4xx; No discount applied to totals; Coupon removal fails; Totals not restored after removal

Title: Shipping API - Get Shipping Methods
Preconditions: Cart with items and shipping address set
Steps: [ACT] Create cart and add item {{TEST_SKU}}; [ACT] PUT shipping address onto cart; [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/cart/carts/{cartId}/availableShippingMethods; [WAIT] Wait for methods list; [ASSERT] Verify method details
Assertions: [FORMAT] Response is array of shipping method objects; [FORMAT] Each method has name, price, currency fields; [FORMAT] Estimated delivery info present; [STATE] At least one shipping method returned
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP status 200; [NETWORK] Methods list non-empty; [ADMIN] Available methods match Admin shipping configuration
Failure_Signals: Empty shipping methods array; Missing name or price on methods; 4xx error; No estimated delivery info

Title: API Error Handling - 404 Not Found
Steps: [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/nonexistent-id-xyz with admin token; [ASSERT] Verify 404 error response structure; [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/order/customerOrders/nonexistent-id-xyz; [ASSERT] Verify 404 error; [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/nonexistent-endpoint; [ASSERT] Verify 404 error
Assertions: [STATE] Product 404 returns 404 status code; [FORMAT] Product 404 response has structured error object (code, message); [STATE] Order 404 returns 404 status code; [FORMAT] Order 404 response has structured error object; [STATE] Unknown endpoint returns 404; [FORMAT] No stack traces in any error response; [FORMAT] Error messages are human-readable
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP 404 for all three requests; [NETWORK] No 500 errors triggered; [CONSOLE] No unhandled server errors
Failure_Signals: 500 Internal Server Error returned instead of 404; Stack trace exposed in response; No error body returned; Error reveals internal file paths

Title: API Error Handling - Validation Errors
Steps: [ACT] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products with empty JSON body {}; [ASSERT] Verify 400 response; [ACT] POST with missing required fields (no name, no code); [ASSERT] Verify validation error details; [ACT] POST with invalid data types (name as integer); [ASSERT] Verify type validation error
Assertions: [STATE] Empty body POST returns 400 Bad Request; [FORMAT] Validation error response lists specific invalid field names; [FORMAT] Error messages are descriptive and actionable; [STATE] No 500 errors returned for any validation scenario
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP 400 for all three requests; [NETWORK] No 500 errors; [CONSOLE] No server exception logs
Failure_Signals: 500 returned for validation issues; Generic 'Bad Request' with no field details; No validation error body; Stack trace in response

Title: API Rate Limiting and Headers
Steps: [ACT] Send 10 rapid sequential GET requests to {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products; [ASSERT] Check rate limit response headers on each response; [ASSERT] Check CORS headers on response; [ASSERT] Check security headers: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options; [ASSERT] Check Server header for version disclosure
Assertions: [FORMAT] Rate limit headers present if rate limiting is configured (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining); [FORMAT] CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin restricted to expected origins only; [FORMAT] X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff present; [STATE] Server version not disclosed in Server or X-Powered-By headers; [FORMAT] Content-Type header correctly set on all responses
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP 200 for all 10 requests (unless rate limit triggered); [NETWORK] Response headers captured for all 10 requests; [CONSOLE] No server errors from rapid requests
Failure_Signals: Server version string in response headers; CORS allows wildcard (*) origin; X-Content-Type-Options absent; 500 errors under rapid load

Title: Platform Modules API - Get Installed Modules
Steps: [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/platform/modules with admin Bearer token; [WAIT] Wait for 200 response; [ASSERT] Verify modules list structure; [ASSERT] Verify required core modules present; [NAV] GET {{BACK_URL}}/health; [WAIT] Wait for health response; [ASSERT] Verify health status
Assertions: [STATE] GET /api/platform/modules returns 200 OK; [FORMAT] Response is array of module objects with id, version, isInstalled fields; [STATE] Core modules (VirtoCommerce.Catalog, VirtoCommerce.Orders, VirtoCommerce.Customer) present and isInstalled=true; [STATE] GET /health returns 200 with healthy status; [FORMAT] Health response includes Modules, Cache, SQL Server statuses
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] HTTP 200 for modules endpoint; [NETWORK] Response includes all core modules; [ADMIN] Module list matches Admin Platform > Modules view; [CONSOLE] No errors
Failure_Signals: Non-200 status for modules endpoint; Core modules absent or isInstalled=false; Health endpoint returns degraded/unhealthy; 401 Unauthorized for admin token
